observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require
the City Accountant to (a) adjust the misstatements in the ensuing year
(CY 2023) through the following entry:
For the erroneous recognition of transfer of unused LDRRMF to STF
Trust Fund -DRRM books:
Accounts and
Account Code Debit Credit
Explanation
Due from Other Funds 1-03-04-050 ₱18,92,576.32
(DRRM)
Accounts Receivable 1-03-01-010 ₱ 18,962,576.32
To record correction of
erroneous
accounting entry
made for the
transfer of CY
2022 unused
LDRRM Fund
and; (b) henceforth, ensure that all unreleased checks as at yearend are
reverted to Cash in Bank balances and the corresponding liabilities
restored.

2.21 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Inventory
Committee to complete the physical count of PPEs and render the
corresponding RPCPPE to the Accounting Office and the Office of Audit
Team; (b) City Accountant and CGSO to (i) continue completing and
updating PPELCs and property cards, respectively; and (ii) regularly
reconcile their PPE records; and (c) convene a Disposal Committee that
shall facilitate the identification and disposition of unserviceable
properties in accordance with the prescribed procedures.

3.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
City Engineer and the Local Development Council to ensure that the (a)
12 unfinished projects are completed as planned; (b) projects proposed
for funding under the 20% DF are procurement and implementation-
ready; and (c) use of the 20% DF is maximized and conforms with the
pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and its implementing rules and
regulations.

4.20 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) City Health
Officer, Provincial General Services Officer and City Accountant to
maintain and reconcile their respective records on vaccine inventories;
and (b) City Health Officer to (i) elevate to the concerned DOH office the
issue on the expired vaccines, or any medicine for that matter, with the
end view of delineating clear responsibilities on its management; and (ii)
continue the close monitoring of the COVID-19 situation in the city and
champion the delivery of support to the DOH vaccination program.

5.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) City Development
Council, City Budget Officer, and all heads of offices to align program
prioritization with reasonably approximated revenues during budget
deliberation; (b) City Assessor to provide reliable basis in estimating
RPTs; and (c) City Treasurer to (i) recommend for the revision of future
BLGF income performance targets, as warranted; and (ii) consider new
initiatives in collection strategies.

6.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the CDRRMO to (a)
continue monitoring the capability of the City to respond to and prepare
for disasters to ensure the people’s sustained resilience towards disasters;
and (b) henceforth ensure that the LDRRMF is optimally utilized.

7.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue mainstreaming gender
equality in policy making and program planning and implementation in
public service.

9.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Human
Resource Management Officer (HRMO) in coordination with the City
Accountant to continuously (a) reconcile records with the GSIS EBCS;
and (b) facilitate coordination between the former City employees and the
GSIS for reconciliation and settlement of prior years’ unremitted
contributions and loan amortizations.

10.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the HRMO to (a)
continuously update and reconcile employees data/record with the EPRS
until all withheld PhilHealth premiums are fully remitted; and (b) sustain
its compliance with the National Health Insurance Act.

11.4 We recommended and the LCE concurred to continuously comply with
the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt
remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments.

are sustained.

1.22 We recommended and the City Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan agreed to
ensure that (a) every constituent is given equal access to any future assistance that the City may provide in accordance with equitable criteria for the selection
of beneficiaries; and (b) the City’s satisfactory responses to COVID-19
pandemic, or to any emerging natural or man-made risks for that matter, are
sustained. Moreover, we advise that the City Mayor instruct the –
a) City Accountant to adjust the balances of the inventories affected through
the following suggested journal entries:
i. For the unissued vaccines as at December 31, 2021:
Drugs and Medicines for Distribution ₱ 12,421,180.85
Prior Year’s Adjustment ₱ 12,421,180.85

ii. For the issuances of inventories made during the year:
Prior Year’s Adjustment ₱ xxxx
Drugs and Medicines for Distribution ₱ xxxx
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies
for Distribution ₱ xxxx
b) CGSO (i) to ensure that all supplies and materials received by the City are
accounted before the same are issued to end-users; and (ii) in coordination
with the CHO and CSWDO, to report to the City Accounting Office the
monthly issuances of inventories using the Monthly Report of Supplies and
Materials Issued (MRSMI); and
c) CHO to ensure accuracy in the capture of details of vaccinees being
uploaded in the DOH website.

2.7 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) require the City Accountant
and CGSO to (i) maintain a complete and updated PPELC and property cards,
respectively, for each class of property; (ii) regularly reconcile their PPE
records; and (iii) coordinate with the DOH for the valuation of the donated PPE
items; (b) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPEs and
render the corresponding RPCPPE in its prescribed form and content; and (c)
convene a Disposal Committee that shall facilitate the timely identification and
disposition of unserviceable properties in accordance with the prescribed
procedures.

3.4 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the (a) City Health
Officer or the Head any other concerned office for that matter, to coordinate
with the CGSO and City Accountant for any donations received by their
respective offices; (b) CGSO to submit to the City Accountant the monthly
report on issuances of inventories (MRSMI); (c) City Accountant to (i) recognize
and disclose in the quarterly and year-end financial statements the balances and
status of donations-in-kind received; and (ii) record the issuances of inventories
based on the MRSMI; and (d) Inventory Committee to render report on the
results of the physical count of inventories.

4.12 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to require the CDRRMC to (a)
draw a plan on the use of available LDRRMF based on an extensive evaluation
of the vulnerability of the City to natural or man-made disaster risks; (b)
henceforth ensure that the LDRRMF is optimally utilized; and (c) sustain the
current effective responses to disasters and its attendant risks.

5.8 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the (a) City Engineer
to ensure that the four unfinished projects are completed as planned; and (b)
Local Development Council (LDC) to continue on finding ways on how to align
future development projects with the demands of the times with requisite
consideration of applicable regulations.

6.7 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the City Accountant
to (a) submit the disbursement vouchers with supporting documents within ten
days of the ensuing month; and (b) henceforth, ensure the timely submission of
all transaction documents.

7.8 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the GAD Focal Person
to consider the viability of the PPAs included in the next year’s GPB considering the
updated situation of the COVID-19 pandemic by then, and take appropriate
actions with the end view of maximizing the use of fund set aside for GAD.

8.7 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to (a) direct the City Treasurer
to continuously identify and recommend measures that will enhance income
collections, with proper consideration of the ongoing pandemic; and (b) direct
the City Budget Officer to strengthen budget estimation procedures so as to
approximate realistic revenue targets.

9.4 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the City Accountant
and the City Treasurer to continue the timely withholding and remittance of
taxes.

10.6 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to instruct the (a) City Human
Resource Management Officer (HRMO) in coordination with the City
Accountant to (i) reconcile records with the GSIS EBCS and take appropriate
action for the disposition of the discrepancies; and (ii) timely inform the GSIS
of the changes in personnel salary data; (b) City Accountant to restore to Due
to GSIS account the remittances that were rejected by EBCS thru the following
entry:
Other Payables ₱xxx
Due to GSIS ₱xxx;
and, (c) HRMO to cause the strict deduction of the billed amounts in the
monthly payrolls.

11.3 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to continuously comply with the
National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of
PhilHealth premiums.

12.4 We recommended and the City Mayor concurred to continuously comply with
the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of
HDMF contributions and loan repayments.

13.2 We commended Management on its continued observance of the laws and
regulations on the receipt and use of government funds.

observations.managementAction:
The City Mayor has already issued directive to the concerned office in accordance with the recommendations of the auditor.






The CHO will report to GSO any inventories officially received from DOH and any other sources to ensure that personal items are removed from the stockroom and to maintain adequate records of supplies and inventories kept by the office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the City Mayor to instruct the: 

(a) City Engineer to ensure that the two unfinished projects are completed as planned; and 

(b) Local Development Council (LDC) to explore ways on how to align future development projects with the demands of the times with requisite consideration of applicable regulations; and 

(c) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to determine the most appropriate mode of procurement that could facilitate the procurement of the City’s development projects even in this time of COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended the City Mayor to: 

(a) sustain its effective responses to disaster-related risks; and 

(b) being on the lookout for any emerging natural or man-made risk and to act on it accordingly especially during this time of health crisis.

We recommended the City Mayor to: 

(a) direct the City Treasurer to identify and recommend measures that will 	enhance income collections, particularly RPT/SET; and 

(b) facilitate 	the completion of the information system for RPT.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to improve its collection but still not come up with the required targets due to the pandemic situation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Mayor to instruct the GAD Focal Person to continue considering the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic in preparing and implementing the GAD Plan 	for CY 2021 and thereafter, or until less prohibitive conditions are restored, to promote gender equality and providing gender-responsive public service.
observations.managementAction: Management still failed to strategize in order to fully accomplish GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE and other City officials concerned to continue in complying with BIR regulations on the withholding and prompt remittance of taxes.
We recommended the LCE and other City Officials concerned to continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291.
We recommended the LCE to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act on the withholding and prompt remittance of PhilHealth premiums.
We recommended the LCE to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments.
We commended Management for its continued observance of the laws and regulations on the receipt and use of government funds.
